cause_analysis.narrative: Nette's Essentials Massage churned on 2026-02-25 after 402 days with Zoca. They sent an explicit cancellation request on 2026-01-06 via sms — 50 days before churn. Classification: explicit cancellation. The client formally asked to cancel; the question is whether a save attempt was made. 2 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days — this is the clearest operational failure on Zoca's side.
recommendations: [Retention] Cancellation was signalled 50 day(s) before churn via sms. Trigger an AM save call within 4 hours of any cancellation keyword — not a support response.
[Offer] Build a 3-tier retention offer ladder (pause / discount / scope change) so AMs have something concrete to counter with.
[SLA] 2 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days. Enforce a hard rule: every inbound gets a human response within 24h, every day of the week.
[SLA example] On 2026-02-23 via phone_call, client wrote: "Hello, I already spoke to somebody this morning, please do not call back.…" — no outbound response in 30 days.
[SLA example] On 2026-03-29 via sms, client wrote: "https://share.icloud.com/photos/0c7yKOH27CkvgsKKnK-3245dQ…" — no outbound response in 30 days.
